title: Clawford Identity Binding
description: Schema for the centralized identity binding that maps usernames to canonical UIDs. Separate from learning credentials — this handles admission-time identity, not academic achievement. UIDs are universal: the same schema covers students and professors.

uid.description: Canonical unique identifier issued by the system. Deterministic from the normalized username via SHA-256. Format: CLW-{16 hex chars}. Immutable once assigned.

username.description: The normalized (trimmed, whitespace-stripped, lowercased) username chosen at registration time. Serves as the primary lookup key.

passwordHash.description: SHA-256 hash of (password + salt). Never exposed in any API response.

salt.description: Random 16-byte hex string generated at registration. Used for password hashing.

displayName.description: User-editable public name. Does not affect UID or transcript integrity.

house.description: Legacy optional field. Canonical house assignment now lives on the learner transcript after foundations sorting.

linkedIds.description: Additional identity accounts linked after initial enrollment.

$comment: Blob storage layout: identity-registry.json maps normalized usernames to identity records. Per-user transcripts live at transcripts/{uid}.json. Final house + verdict are written to transcript after foundations completion. The public student-wall.json index is rebuilt on each transcript write. Write authority: only the server may write transcripts, identity records, and wall indexes. Users may update only their own displayName via the PATCH /api/transcript endpoint. The passwordHash and salt fields are never exposed in API responses.
